# Programming Service Agreement

**Effective date:** [Date]  
**Client:** [Client name]  
**Provider:** [Provider/Company name]  
**Product:** LockIn Protocol (as described in the Product Requirements Document)

---

## 1. What This Agreement Covers

This agreement sets out the terms for custom software development and related services for the LockIn Protocol application. The work is defined by the Product Requirements Document (PRD) and any agreed change orders. Both parties agree to these terms when they sign or start work.

---

## 2. Scope of Work

- The Provider will design, build, test, and deliver the software and systems described in the PRD (smart contracts, frontend, backend, and integrations).
- Deliverables include: source code, deployment instructions, and documentation as specified in the PRD.
- Anything not listed in the PRD or a change order is out of scope unless both parties agree in writing.

---

## 3. Client Responsibilities

- The Client will provide timely feedback, access to accounts and systems (e.g. wallets, testnet funds, API keys) when needed, and any content or branding as agreed.
- The Client is responsible for legal, compliance, and regulatory matters (e.g. KYC, disclosures, data protection in their jurisdiction). The Provider builds the system as specified and does not provide legal advice.
- The Client will arrange and fund the security audit of smart contracts before mainnet, as required by the PRD.

---

## 4. Payment and Schedule

- Payment amount, schedule, and method (e.g. milestone-based, monthly) will be set in a separate order or statement of work.
- Invoices are due within [e.g. 30] days unless otherwise agreed. Late payment may result in pause of work after written notice.
- Out-of-scope work or change orders will be quoted separately and must be approved in writing before the Provider performs it.

---

## 5. Intellectual Property and Ownership

- Upon full payment for the work, the Client will own the custom deliverables (source code, documentation) created specifically for LockIn Protocol under this agreement.
- The Provider keeps ownership of its pre-existing tools, libraries, and methods. The Client receives a license to use them as part of the delivered product.
- Open-source components used in the project remain under their respective licenses; the Provider will identify them and ensure compatibility with delivery.

---

## 6. Confidentiality

- Each party will keep the other’s confidential information (e.g. business data, technical details) secret and use it only for this project, unless required by law or with the other party’s consent.
- This obligation continues after the agreement ends for information that remains confidential by its nature.

---

## 7. Warranties and Limitations

- The Provider warrants that the work will be performed in a professional manner and will substantially conform to the PRD when delivered. The Provider will correct material defects that are reported in writing within a defined warranty period (e.g. 30 days after delivery) at no extra charge.
- Except as stated above, the software and services are provided “as is.” The Provider does not warrant uninterrupted or error-free operation, and is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages (e.g. lost profits, loss of data), or for damages beyond the total amount paid by the Client under this agreement for the relevant project.
- The Provider is not liable for how the Client or third parties use the software (e.g. mainnet use before audit, misuse of keys, or regulatory non-compliance).

---

## 8. Termination

- Either party may terminate this agreement with written notice. The Client will pay for all work completed and non-cancellable costs up to the termination date.
- Sections that by their nature should survive (e.g. payment, confidentiality, limitations of liability, ownership) will remain in effect after termination.

---

## 9. General

- **Changes:** Changes to scope or terms must be in writing (e.g. change order or signed amendment).
- **Entire agreement:** This document and the PRD (and any signed change orders) form the full agreement for this programming service. Prior discussions or drafts do not override these terms.
- **Governing law:** [Specify jurisdiction, e.g. State/Country] law applies. Any disputes will be resolved in the courts of [specified location] unless both parties agree otherwise.
- **Notices:** Notices under this agreement will be sent in writing to the addresses (or email) agreed by both parties.

---

## 10. Sign-off

By signing below, both parties agree to this Programming Service Agreement and to the scope described in the PRD.
